overall_summary: Digital Power Network, a coalition of Bitcoin miners representing most of the domestic hash rate, broadly supports FERC expediting interconnection of flexible large loads and works through the reform principles point by point. It backs joint load-and-generation study, co-location incentives, standardized study deposits credited back to developers, expedited 60-day study for curtailable facilities, and a NERC flexible-load category, while cautioning that DOE's jurisdictional grounding is legally contestable and that transmission owners need accountability for study delays.

quotes.text: FERC’s support for expediting the integration of flexible loads is critical to bolster the nation’s power systems.
quotes.concern: Supports FERC action to speed integration of flexible loads

quotes.text: the Commission’s jurisdiction should be limited to interconnections directly to transmission facilities, consistent with the Commission’s seven-factor test.
quotes.concern: Wants FERC jurisdiction confined to direct transmission interconnections

quotes.text: The NARUC resolution signals that state regulators nationwide are opposed to the Department of Energy's effort to enable FERC to supplant states' authority over large load interconnections.
quotes.concern: Flags state-regulator opposition and contestable DOE jurisdiction

quotes.text: in certain cases, it should expand to facilities below 20 MW given that hydro facilities can often fall under this capacity limit.
quotes.concern: Wants threshold exceptions below 20 MW for smaller facilities

quotes.text: load and hybrid facilities should be studied together with generating facilities. Such an approach will allow for efficient siting of loads and generating facilities and thereby minimize the need for costly network upgrades.
quotes.concern: Supports joint study of load and generation to cut upgrades

quotes.text: Study deposits would assist in solving the problem of entities flooding utilities with requests for load.
quotes.concern: Supports study deposits to deter speculative load requests

quotes.text: Given that data centers are not subject to paying these deposits now, these payments should be credited back over the first year of the contract.
quotes.concern: Wants study deposits credited back to developers

quotes.text: Hybrid facilities need to have the ability for future revisions to their injection and / or withdrawal rights as the transmission system capacity increases / improves.
quotes.concern: Wants revisable injection and withdrawal rights for hybrids

quotes.text: This provides incentives for co-location with new generation facilities and ensures efficient buildout of the transmission system.
quotes.concern: Backs co-location of load with new generation

quotes.text: any hybrid interconnection shall be required to install the system protection facilities necessary to prevent unauthorized injections or withdrawals that exceed the respective rights.
quotes.concern: Supports system protection facilities for hybrid interconnections

quotes.text: FERC should also require that flexible loads have non-firm withdrawal rights, ensuring that excess load on the grid can be monetized during period of low demand
quotes.concern: Wants non-firm withdrawal rights for flexible loads

quotes.text: the interconnection study of large loads that agree to be curtailable and hybrid facilities that agree to be curtailable and dispatchable should be expedited.
quotes.concern: Supports expedited study for curtailable, dispatchable facilities

quotes.text: There also needs to be an even higher priority for company submissions that have proven their ability to curtail / dispatched at their facilities vs. new entries.
quotes.concern: Wants priority for proven curtailable operators in expedited queue

quotes.text: the upgrade costs need to be credited back to the facility over the course the power purchase agreement (PPA) length. The maximum duration for issuing the credit should be 50% of the PPA length.
quotes.concern: Accepts 100% upgrade cost but seeks crediting over PPA

quotes.text: This prevents the transmission owner from having a monopoly on construction that could delay projects or raise costs.
quotes.concern: Supports interconnection customer option to build

quotes.text: NERC should have a flexible load category. Reliability standards should emphasize grid resilience on the supply side and be transparent, achievable, market-based solutions on the demand side.
quotes.concern: Wants a NERC flexible-load category and demand-side standards

quotes.text: there should be fines imposed or some accountability mechanism for TO’s and TSPs to complete the studies and their part of the process on time.
quotes.concern: Wants penalties holding transmission owners accountable for delays

quotes.text: There must be a clear pathway to interconnect non-firm, flexible large load- SPP is already considering a process called PALS so examples exists.
quotes.concern: Cites SPP PALS as a non-firm flexible-load pathway model

bins.name: Expedite flexible large loads
bins.description: DPN calls FERC support for integrating flexible loads critical and pushes for non-firm withdrawal rights, expedited study for curtailable facilities, and clear non-firm load pathways. Flexibility is the core value it wants the rule to reward.

bins.name: Limit FERC jurisdiction
bins.description: DPN supports confining FERC jurisdiction to direct transmission interconnections under the seven-factor test and warns that DOE's grounding is legally contestable, citing a NARUC resolution opposing federal preemption of state authority over large loads.

bins.name: Allow sub-20 MW exceptions
bins.description: DPN treats 20 MW as an adequate baseline but wants exceptions for smaller facilities such as hydro that fall below the limit, so developers can start small and expand.

bins.name: Joint load and generation study
bins.description: DPN backs studying load and hybrid facilities together with generation, arguing it enables efficient siting and reduces the need for costly network upgrades.

bins.name: Co-study load with generation
bins.description: DPN supports the joint-study approach in the ANOPR, pointing to AI firms re-energizing idled generation and siting data centers nearby so loads are not studied in isolation.

bins.name: Study deposits credited back
bins.description: DPN supports standardized study deposits to deter speculative requests that flood utilities, but wants them non-trivial yet not excessive and credited back over the first contract year.

bins.name: Revisable hybrid rights
bins.description: DPN wants hybrid facilities able to revise their injection and withdrawal rights as transmission capacity improves, preserving future growth in either direction.

bins.name: Incentivize co-location
bins.description: DPN favors studying hybrids by the injection and withdrawal rights requested because it incentivizes co-location with new generation and efficient transmission buildout.

bins.name: Hybrid system protection
bins.description: DPN supports requiring system protection facilities on hybrid interconnections to prevent unauthorized injections or withdrawals beyond the granted rights.

bins.name: Expedited curtailable study
bins.description: DPN calls expedited study for curtailable and dispatchable facilities essential and wants even higher priority for operators with a proven curtailment and dispatch track record over new entrants.

bins.name: Upgrade cost with crediting
bins.description: DPN accepts that loads bear their assigned network upgrade costs but wants those costs credited back over the PPA, capped at half the PPA length, and flags open questions about infrastructure ownership and tax credits.

bins.name: Cost crediting and build option
bins.description: DPN seeks crediting of network upgrade costs over the PPA and an option for the interconnection customer to build, preventing a transmission-owner construction monopoly that could delay projects or raise costs.

bins.name: NERC flexible-load category
bins.description: DPN urges NERC to create a flexible-load category and to keep demand-side reliability standards transparent, achievable, and market-based rather than tools to slow data-center interconnection.

bins.name: Transmission owner accountability
bins.description: DPN argues transmission owner delay is a major bottleneck and wants fines or other accountability so transmission owners and providers complete their studies on time, replacing the current good-faith model.

bins.name: SPP PALS as model
bins.description: DPN points to SPP's PALS process as an existing example of a clear pathway to interconnect non-firm, flexible large load.
